clusters.failureShape: A validation error is shown visually but is not programmatically tied to its field — the input lacks aria-invalid and aria-describedby — and often isn't announced, so screen-reader users learn an entry is wrong but not which field or why.
clusters.fix: Mark the failing input aria-invalid="true" and point aria-describedby at the error message element, and surface the message in a live region. Identify the field and the reason in text.

clusters.failureShape: Validation is wrong or sticky — an error persists after the input becomes valid, or the form stays in an invalid state and blocks submission even with correct data — so the user can't recover or proceed.
clusters.fix: Clear validation errors as soon as the input becomes valid and re-evaluate state on change, so a corrected field no longer blocks submission or keeps announcing a stale error.

clusters.failureShape: On a failed submit, focus stays on the submit button instead of moving to the first invalid field, so keyboard and screen-reader users must search the form to find what went wrong.
clusters.fix: On validation failure, move focus to the first erroneous field so the user lands directly on the problem.
